title: PSC002845: RFI for a Self-Service Contract Management System (CMS)
summary:
ANSTO is a corporate Commonwealth entity and Australiaâs nuclear research and technology organisation. ANSTO is responsible for delivering specialised advice, scientific services and products to government, industry, academia and other research organisations. It is our mission to deliver knowledge, value and trust through the application of nuclear science, technology and engineering.

ANSTO has a fully implemented contract management system that has been in place for circa 5 years.  ANSTO seeks to review what is available in the market to determine its planning for a software-as-a-service solution (ie not a bespoke build) contract management system in future years and to understand the technology options available.

ANSTO seeks information regarding suitably qualified and experienced Suppliers of, and their plan to deliver, a contract management system that meets the requirements ANSTO has currently identified below (the System).

 

The details sought for the System are comprised of:

a)    Details on functionality and capacity, and options for implementation (including data migration capability) and information on data hosting, security and management, including cyber security, by reference to the requirements set out below;

b)    Indicative pricing for an off the shelf product; and

c)     Additional information about the Supplier.

These can be provided by completing Part B and the table in this Part C.

This is not an approach to market at this stage.  This is a request for information to gather information about what is available, and a mechanism for ANSTO to understand suitable contract management systems available for a corporate Commonwealth entity in Australia.

If you choose not to respond to this request, you may still respond to any future open approach to market by ANSTO for a contract management system.  Please be aware that ANSTO may use information provided in this RFI including to inform future planning including development of requirements in a future approach to market (which may change from this RFI) and budgeting, and as otherwise outlined in Part D of this RFI.

title: DHA-PROC26/572: Expression of Interest - Furnishings, Storage, Logistics and Associated Services
summary:
DHA has a requirement to fully furnish multiple properties in our portfolio. This includes provision of goods, necessary logistics, temporary storage, and inventory management in between furniture usage.  

 DHA are seeking suppliers capable of delivering the following goods and services: 

1.      Providing furniture, electrical items and homewares (the furnishings).

2.      Providing associated services including delivery, assembly and staging of furnishings and inventory management and stock control (the services).

3.      Provision of logistic and storage services including packing, collection, storage and re-delivery (logistics and storage) for some of the furnishings.

Collectively, all elements are referred to as the requirement.

Upon registration and Deed of Confidentiality execution, an Information Pack can be provided to Respondents with further details.

title: ATM_2026_6621: Sediment Monitoring Program - Maugean Skate Program
summary: The Department is seeking a provider to design and implement a field-validated Methodology for: quantifying the oxygen demand from sediment in Macquarie Harbour, and assess the drivers and context of oxygen demand; updating the bathymetry of key areas of Macquarie Harbour; determining, and possibly implementing, an appropriate Method for investigating Maugean skate prey species; and consolidating relevant environmental datasets The work will include the collection of sediment cores and associated samples across multiple sites in a manner that is statistically representative of the harbour, with repeat sampling of a subset of sites to assess seasonal influences. The work will require the design of a Methodology, consisting of a number of Methods, and its implementation through the collection and laboratory analysis of organic and inorganic sediment parameters, including assessment of sediment composition and microbial activity. The objective is to generate a comprehensive and integrated understanding of the sediments and benthic processes of Macquarie Harbour - in particular a scientifically defensible estimate of benthic oxygen demand across Macquarie Harbour, including the identification of areas with elevated oxygen demand arising from a number of possible sources; to enhance understanding of Macquarie Harbourâs physical environment through high-resolution bathymetry - in particular the dynamic environment of mouth of the Harbour -, if possible, to assess the historic and current availability of key Maugean skate prey species, and to support broader program analyses and the National Recovery Team for the Maugean skate.

raw.title: ATM_2026_6621: Sediment Monitoring Program - Maugean Skate Program
raw.description: The Department is seeking a provider to design and implement a field-validated Methodology for: quantifying the oxygen demand from sediment in Macquarie Harbour, and assess the drivers and context of oxygen demand; updating the bathymetry of key areas of Macquarie Harbour; determining, and possibly implementing, an appropriate Method for investigating Maugean skate prey species; and consolidating relevant environmental datasets The work will include the collection of sediment cores and associated samples across multiple sites in a manner that is statistically representative of the harbour, with repeat sampling of a subset of sites to assess seasonal influences. The work will require the design of a Methodology, consisting of a number of Methods, and its implementation through the collection and laboratory analysis of organic and inorganic sediment parameters, including assessment of sediment composition and microbial activity. The objective is to generate a comprehensive and integrated understanding of the sediments and benthic processes of Macquarie Harbour - in particular a scientifically defensible estimate of benthic oxygen demand across Macquarie Harbour, including the identification of areas with elevated oxygen demand arising from a number of possible sources; to enhance understanding of Macquarie Harbourâs physical environment through high-resolution bathymetry - in particular the dynamic environment of mouth of the Harbour -, if possible, to assess the historic and current availability of key Maugean skate prey species, and to support broader program analyses and the National Recovery Team for the Maugean skate.

title: NRD07664: Charles Perkins House Lighting Upgrade
summary:
The lighting system at Charles Perkins House (CPH) is at end of life, unserviceable, and no longer compliant with current safety and performance standards.    

The existing lighting system is Clipsal Schneider DALI, as per the specifications in the Approach to Market document.    

The full LED upgrade must:

â¢ Ensure compliance with workplace safety and illumination standards.

â¢ Support the NIAAâs Net Zero targets through energy-efficient infrastructure.

â¢ Achieve recommended lux levels across all workstations.

title: 10031970: Area Packages 1 and 3 (AP1/3)
summary:
The Australian Government is planning a future HSR network to connect Brisbane, Sydney, Canberra, Melbourne and regional communities across the east coast of Australia.

The Authority is the Australian Governmentâs principal adviser on the development of HSR. Its purpose is to develop a nationally integrated HSR network, connecting cities and key regional centres, enhancing Australiaâs long-term rail investment through policy development, national coordination, and strategic planning.

title: DHAPROC26/311: Asphalt and Line Marking Stages 2 to 6, Nirimba Fields
summary:
Request for Tender (RFT) covers the supply and installation of Asphalt and Line marking across Stages 2 to 6 Nirimba Fields, Blacktown, NSW  

Please Note: Defence Housing Australia (DHA) is not required to comply with the Commonwealth Procurement Framework, including the Commonwealth Procurement Rules (CPRs).  DHA is not a prescribed Corporate Commonwealth entity for the purposes of the CPRs under Section 30 of the Public Governance, Performance and Accountability Rule 2014.  Given this, other aspects of the RFT tender process, may not (and are not required to) be consistent with the CPRs. This RFT is issued through AusTender to leverage a convenient and efficient tendering platform and provide notification of the opportunity to suppliers
